title: Pleurothyrium palmanum
taxonomy: Pleurothyrium palmanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This family is commonly known as the custard apple family. Within the Annonaceae family, Pleurothyrium palmanum belongs to the genus Pleurothyrium. The genus Pleurothyrium is a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The classification of Pleurothyrium palmanum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Pleurothyrium palmanum is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Lauraceae and known for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Pleurothyrium palmanum include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, bronchitis, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, particularly the compounds palmarumycin and pleurothyriol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pleurothyrium palmanum are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine in the Amazon region to treat various ailments.
